It was originally called the {{nihongo|Hatomori Hachimangū|鳩森八幡宮||}}. The shrine was rebuilt in 1593 by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] to be the protective shrine for [[:en:Morioka Castle|Morioka Castle]]. Under the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] system of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|shrine Ranking]] from 1871 through 1946, the Morioka Hachimangū was officially designated as a "prefectural shrine". The Edo-period shrine structures burned down in 1884. The present main structure dates from 2006. ==Shinto belief== The shrine is dedicated to the veneration of the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' [[Hachiman]]. Hachiman has been recognized as an amalgamation of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]] and his consort, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]].Ponsbonby-Fane, ''Studies,'' pp. 78, 196. ==See also== * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2362-7}}; {{OCLC|43487317}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC| 399449}} ==External links== {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} * {{official website|http://www.morioka8man.jp/ }} {{in lang|ja}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{translated page|ja|盛岡八幡宮|version=101918390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1062 establishments in Asia]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:Morioka, Iwate]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] }} [[en:Morioka Hachimangū]] [[ja:盛岡八幡宮]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Morioka]] [[Category:History of Morioka]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]] {{Commons category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} [[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]] {{Commons category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} [[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11581351}}

[[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]][[commons:Category:Morioka-Hachimangū]][[en:Morioka Hachimangū]][[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]][[ja:盛岡八幡宮]][[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]][[commons:Category:Morioka-Hachimangū]][[en:Morioka Hachimangū]][[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]][[ja:盛岡八幡宮]][[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]][[sn:commons:Category:Morioka-Hachimangū]][[en:Morioka Hachimangū]][[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]][[ja:盛岡八幡宮]][[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11581351]] Wikidata name: Morioka Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Morioka Hachiman Shrine
盛岡八幡宮 | image = 171104 Morioka-Hachimangu Morioka Iwate pref Japan04s3.jpg | caption = Morioka Hachimangu Honden | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|39|41|43.11|N|141|09|50.62|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] | founded_by = | established = 1062 | date_destroyed = | location = Yawata-cho 13-2, Morioka, Iwate | website = {{URL|http://www.morioka8man.jp/}} | architecture_style = [[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]] | festival = September 15 | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Morioka Hachimangū'''|盛岡八幡宮}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[:en:Morioka, Iwate|Morioka, Iwate]] in northern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine is noted for its [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] on the second Saturday in June, which is famous for the ''[[:en:Chagu Chagu Umakko|Chagu Chagu Umakko]]'', a horse parade which was recognized in 1978 as an [[:en:Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].{{cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/312/198 |title=Database of National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=18 April 2011}} In 1996 the sound of the bells of the ''Chagu Chagu Umakko'' was selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the [[:en:100 Soundscapes of Japan|100 Soundscapes of Japan]].{{cite web |url=http://www.env.go.jp/air/life/oto/ |title=北海道・東北 - チャグチャグ馬コの鈴の音 |publisher=[[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] |accessdate=18 April 2011 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120608002719/http://www.env.go.jp/air/life/oto/ |archivedate=8 June 2012 }} The shrine is also noted for its displays of ''[[yabusame]]'' horse archery during its annual festival on September 15. ==History== The Morioka Hachimangū was established in 1062 during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] brought a ''[[bunrei]]'' of the [[Minamoto clan]]’s tutelary shrine, the [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] in Kyoto to pray for victory in his campaign against the [[Abe clan]] in the [[:en:Former Nine Years War|Former Nine Years War]]. It was originally called the {{nihongo|Hatomori Hachimangū|鳩森八幡宮||}}. The shrine was rebuilt in 1593 by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] to be the protective shrine for [[:en:Morioka Castle|Morioka Castle]]. Under the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] system of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|shrine Ranking]] from 1871 through 1946, the Morioka Hachimangū was officially designated as a "prefectural shrine". The Edo-period shrine structures burned down in 1884. The present main structure dates from 2006. ==Shinto belief== The shrine is dedicated to the veneration of the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' [[Hachiman]]. Hachiman has been recognized as an amalgamation of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]] and his consort, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]].Ponsbonby-Fane, ''Studies,'' pp. 78, 196. ==See also== * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2362-7}}; {{OCLC|43487317}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC| 399449}} ==External links== {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} * {{official website|http://www.morioka8man.jp/ }} {{in lang|ja}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{translated page|ja|盛岡八幡宮|version=101918390|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Morioka Hachimangū]] [[ja:盛岡八幡宮]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]] {{Commons category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} [[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]] {{Commons category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} [[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] {{wikidata link|Q11581351}} [[Category:1062 establishments in Asia]] [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Morioka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Morioka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Morioka, Iwate]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

盛岡八幡宮 | image = 171104 Morioka-Hachimangu Morioka Iwate pref Japan04s3.jpg | caption = Morioka Hachimangu Honden | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|39|41|43.11|N|141|09|50.62|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] | founded_by = | established = 1062 | date_destroyed = | location = Yawata-cho 13-2, Morioka, Iwate | website = {{URL|http://www.morioka8man.jp/}} | architecture_style = [[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]] | festival = September 15 | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Morioka Hachimangū'''|盛岡八幡宮}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in the city of [[:en:Morioka, Iwate|Morioka, Iwate]] in northern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine is noted for its [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] on the second Saturday in June, which is famous for the ''[[:en:Chagu Chagu Umakko|Chagu Chagu Umakko]]'', a horse parade which was recognized in 1978 as an [[:en:Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].{{cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/312/198 |title=Database of National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=18 April 2011}} In 1996 the sound of the bells of the ''Chagu Chagu Umakko'' was selected by the [[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] as one of the [[:en:100 Soundscapes of Japan|100 Soundscapes of Japan]].{{cite web |url=http://www.env.go.jp/air/life/oto/ |title=北海道・東北 - チャグチャグ馬コの鈴の音 |publisher=[[:en:Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|Ministry of the Environment]] |accessdate=18 April 2011 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120608002719/http://www.env.go.jp/air/life/oto/ |archivedate=8 June 2012 }} The shrine is also noted for its displays of ''[[yabusame]]'' horse archery during its annual festival on September 15. ==History== The Morioka Hachimangū was established in 1062 during the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] brought a ''[[bunrei]]'' of the [[Minamoto clan]]’s tutelary shrine, the [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] in Kyoto to pray for victory in his campaign against the [[Abe clan]] in the [[:en:Former Nine Years War|Former Nine Years War]]. It was originally called the {{nihongo|Hatomori Hachimangū|鳩森八幡宮||}}. The shrine was rebuilt in 1593 by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] to be the protective shrine for [[:en:Morioka Castle|Morioka Castle]]. Under the [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] system of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|shrine Ranking]] from 1871 through 1946, the Morioka Hachimangū was officially designated as a "prefectural shrine". The Edo-period shrine structures burned down in 1884. The present main structure dates from 2006. ==Shinto belief== The shrine is dedicated to the veneration of the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' [[Hachiman]]. Hachiman has been recognized as an amalgamation of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]] and his consort, [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]].Ponsbonby-Fane, ''Studies,'' pp. 78, 196. ==See also== * [[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2362-7}}; {{OCLC|43487317}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]]. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC| 399449}} ==External links== {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} * {{official website|http://www.morioka8man.jp/ }} {{in lang|ja}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{translated page|ja|盛岡八幡宮|version=101918390|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Morioka Hachimangū]] [[ja:盛岡八幡宮]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]] {{Commons category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} [[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]] {{Commons category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} [[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] {{wikidata link|Q11581351}} [[Category:1062 establishments in Asia]] [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Morioka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Morioka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Morioka, Iwate]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

盛岡八幡宮 | image = 171104 Morioka-Hachimangu Morioka Iwate pref Japan04s3.jpg | caption = Morioka Hachimangu Honden | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|39|41|43.11|N|141|09|50.62|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] | founded_by = | established = 1062 | date_destroyed = | location = Yawata-cho 13-2, Morioka, Iwate | website = {{URL|http://www.morioka8man.jp/}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri}} | festival = September 15 | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Morioka Hachimangū'''|盛岡八幡宮}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in the city of [[:en:Morioka, Iwate|Morioka, Iwate]] in northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The shrine is noted for its [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] on the second Saturday in June, which is famous for the ''{{ill|Chagu Chagu Umakko|en|Chagu Chagu Umakko|WD=Q289513|lt=Chagu Chagu Umakko}}'', a horse parade which was recognized in 1978 as an [[:en:Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].{{cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/312/198 |title=Database of National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=18 April 2011}} In 1996 the sound of the bells of the ''Chagu Chagu Umakko'' was selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|en|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|WD=Q1125558|lt=Ministry of the Environment}} as one of the {{ill|100 Soundscapes of Japan|en|100 Soundscapes of Japan|WD=Q1010668|lt=100 Soundscapes of Japan}}.{{cite web |url=http://www.env.go.jp/air/life/oto/ |title=北海道・東北 - チャグチャグ馬コの鈴の音 |publisher={{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|en|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|WD=Q1125558|lt=Ministry of the Environment}} |accessdate=18 April 2011 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120608002719/http://www.env.go.jp/air/life/oto/ |archivedate=8 June 2012 }} The shrine is also noted for its displays of ''[[yabusame]]'' horse archery during its annual festival on September 15. ==History== The Morioka Hachimangū was established in 1062 during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} when [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] brought a ''[[bunrei]]'' of the [[Minamoto clan]]’s tutelary shrine, the [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] in Kyoto to pray for victory in his campaign against the [[Abe clan]] in the [[:en:Former Nine Years War|Former Nine Years War]]. It was originally called the {{nihongo|Hatomori Hachimangū|鳩森八幡宮||}}. The shrine was rebuilt in 1593 by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|en|Nanbu clan|WD=Q2975330|lt=Nanbu clan}} to be the protective shrine for {{ill|Morioka Castle|en|Morioka Castle|WD=Q251650|lt=Morioka Castle}}. Under the {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}} system of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|shrine Ranking]] from 1871 through 1946, the Morioka Hachimangū was officially designated as a "prefectural shrine". The Edo-period shrine structures burned down in 1884. The present main structure dates from 2006. ==Shinto belief== The shrine is dedicated to the veneration of the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' [[Hachiman]]. Hachiman has been recognized as an amalgamation of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]] and his consort, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}.Ponsbonby-Fane, ''Studies,'' pp. 78, 196. ==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2362-7}}; {{OCLC|43487317}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard}}. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard}}. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC| 399449}} ==External links== {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} * {{official website|http://www.morioka8man.jp/ }} {{in lang|ja}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{translated page|ja|盛岡八幡宮|version=101918390|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Morioka Hachimangū]] [[ja:盛岡八幡宮]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]] {{Commons category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} [[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]] {{Commons category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} [[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] {{wikidata link|Q11581351}} [[Category:1062 establishments in Asia]] [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Morioka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Morioka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Morioka, Iwate]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]][[co:commons:Category:Morioka-Hachimangū]][[en:Morioka Hachimangū]][[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]][[ja:盛岡八幡宮]][[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]][[co:commons:Category:Morioka-Hachimangū]][[en:Morioka Hachimangū]][[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]][[ja:盛岡八幡宮]][[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]][[sn:commons:Category:Morioka-Hachimangū]][[en:Morioka Hachimangū]][[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]][[ja:盛岡八幡宮]][[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Morioka Hachiman Shrine
盛岡八幡宮 | image = 171104 Morioka-Hachimangu Morioka Iwate pref Japan04s3.jpg | caption = Morioka Hachimangu Honden | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|39|41|43.11|N|141|09|50.62|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] | founded_by = | established = 1062 | date_destroyed = | location = Yawata-cho 13-2, Morioka, Iwate | website = {{URL|http://www.morioka8man.jp/}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri}} | festival = September 15 | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Morioka Hachimangū'''|盛岡八幡宮}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in the city of [[:en:Morioka, Iwate|Morioka, Iwate]] in northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The shrine is noted for its [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] on the second Saturday in June, which is famous for the ''{{ill|Chagu Chagu Umakko|en|Chagu Chagu Umakko|WD=Q289513|lt=Chagu Chagu Umakko}}'', a horse parade which was recognized in 1978 as an [[:en:Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].{{cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/312/198 |title=Database of National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=18 April 2011}} In 1996 the sound of the bells of the ''Chagu Chagu Umakko'' was selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|en|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|WD=Q1125558|lt=Ministry of the Environment}} as one of the {{ill|100 Soundscapes of Japan|en|100 Soundscapes of Japan|WD=Q1010668|lt=100 Soundscapes of Japan}}.{{cite web |url=http://www.env.go.jp/air/life/oto/ |title=北海道・東北 - チャグチャグ馬コの鈴の音 |publisher={{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|en|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|WD=Q1125558|lt=Ministry of the Environment}} |accessdate=18 April 2011 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120608002719/http://www.env.go.jp/air/life/oto/ |archivedate=8 June 2012 }} The shrine is also noted for its displays of ''[[yabusame]]'' horse archery during its annual festival on September 15. ==History== The Morioka Hachimangū was established in 1062 during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} when [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] brought a ''[[bunrei]]'' of the [[Minamoto clan]]’s tutelary shrine, the [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] in Kyoto to pray for victory in his campaign against the [[Abe clan]] in the [[:en:Former Nine Years War|Former Nine Years War]]. It was originally called the {{nihongo|Hatomori Hachimangū|鳩森八幡宮||}}. The shrine was rebuilt in 1593 by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|en|Nanbu clan|WD=Q2975330|lt=Nanbu clan}} to be the protective shrine for {{ill|Morioka Castle|en|Morioka Castle|WD=Q251650|lt=Morioka Castle}}. Under the {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}} system of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|shrine Ranking]] from 1871 through 1946, the Morioka Hachimangū was officially designated as a "prefectural shrine". The Edo-period shrine structures burned down in 1884. The present main structure dates from 2006. ==Shinto belief== The shrine is dedicated to the veneration of the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' [[Hachiman]]. Hachiman has been recognized as an amalgamation of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]] and his consort, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}.Ponsbonby-Fane, ''Studies,'' pp. 78, 196. ==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2362-7}}; {{OCLC|43487317}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard}}. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard}}. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC| 399449}} ==External links== {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} * {{official website|http://www.morioka8man.jp/ }} {{in lang|ja}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{translated page|ja|盛岡八幡宮|version=101918390|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Morioka Hachimangū]] [[ja:盛岡八幡宮]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]] {{Commons category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} [[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]] {{Commons category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} [[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] {{wikidata link|Q11581351}} [[Category:1062 establishments in Asia]] [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Morioka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Morioka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Morioka, Iwate]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC| 399449}} ==External links== {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} * {{official website|http://www.morioka8man.jp/ }} {{in lang|ja}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{translated page|ja|盛岡八幡宮|version=101918390|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Morioka Hachimangū]] [[ja:盛岡八幡宮]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]] {{Commons category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} [[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]] {{Commons category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} [[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] {{wikidata link|Q11581351}} [[Category:1062 establishments in Asia]] [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Morioka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Morioka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Morioka, Iwate]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]][[co:commons:Category:Morioka-Hachimangū]][[en:Morioka Hachimangū]][[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]][[ja:盛岡八幡宮]][[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]][[co:commons:Category:Morioka-Hachimangū]][[en:Morioka Hachimangū]][[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]][[ja:盛岡八幡宮]][[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]][[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Morioka-Hachimangū]][[en:Morioka Hachimangū]][[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]][[ja:盛岡八幡宮]][[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Morioka Hachiman Shrine
盛岡八幡宮 | image = 171104 Morioka-Hachimangu Morioka Iwate pref Japan04s3.jpg | caption = Morioka Hachimangu Honden | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|39|41|43.11|N|141|09|50.62|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] | founded_by = | established = 1062 | date_destroyed = | location = Yawata-cho 13-2, Morioka, Iwate | website = {{URL|http://www.morioka8man.jp/}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri|1=Hachiman-zukuri}} | festival = September 15 | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Morioka Hachimangū'''|盛岡八幡宮}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the city of [[:en:Morioka, Iwate|Morioka, Iwate]] in northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The shrine is noted for its [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] on the second Saturday in June, which is famous for the ''{{ill|Chagu Chagu Umakko|en|Chagu Chagu Umakko|WD=Q289513|lt=Chagu Chagu Umakko|1=Chagu Chagu Umakko}}'', a horse parade which was recognized in 1978 as an [[:en:Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].{{cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/312/198 |title=Database of National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=18 April 2011}} In 1996 the sound of the bells of the ''Chagu Chagu Umakko'' was selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|en|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|WD=Q1125558|lt=Ministry of the Environment|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)}} as one of the {{ill|100 Soundscapes of Japan|en|100 Soundscapes of Japan|WD=Q1010668|lt=100 Soundscapes of Japan|1=100 Soundscapes of Japan}}.{{cite web |url=http://www.env.go.jp/air/life/oto/ |title=北海道・東北 - チャグチャグ馬コの鈴の音 |publisher={{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|en|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|WD=Q1125558|lt=Ministry of the Environment|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)}} |accessdate=18 April 2011 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120608002719/http://www.env.go.jp/air/life/oto/ |archivedate=8 June 2012 }} The shrine is also noted for its displays of ''[[yabusame]]'' horse archery during its annual festival on September 15. ==History== The Morioka Hachimangū was established in 1062 during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} when [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] brought a ''[[bunrei]]'' of the [[Minamoto clan]]’s tutelary shrine, the [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] in Kyoto to pray for victory in his campaign against the [[Abe clan]] in the [[:en:Former Nine Years War|Former Nine Years War]]. It was originally called the {{nihongo|Hatomori Hachimangū|鳩森八幡宮||}}. The shrine was rebuilt in 1593 by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|en|Nanbu clan|WD=Q2975330|lt=Nanbu clan|1=Nanbu clan}} to be the protective shrine for {{ill|Morioka Castle|en|Morioka Castle|WD=Q251650|lt=Morioka Castle|1=Morioka Castle}}. Under the {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}} system of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|shrine Ranking]] from 1871 through 1946, the Morioka Hachimangū was officially designated as a "prefectural shrine". The Edo-period shrine structures burned down in 1884. The present main structure dates from 2006. ==Shinto belief== The shrine is dedicated to the veneration of the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' [[Hachiman]]. Hachiman has been recognized as an amalgamation of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]] and his consort, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}.Ponsbonby-Fane, ''Studies,'' pp. 78, 196. ==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2362-7}}; {{OCLC|43487317}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC| 399449}} ==External links== {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} * {{official website|http://www.morioka8man.jp/ }} {{in lang|ja}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{translated page|ja|盛岡八幡宮|version=101918390|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Morioka Hachimangū]] [[ja:盛岡八幡宮]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]] {{Commons category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} [[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]] {{Commons category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} [[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] {{wikidata link|Q11581351}} [[Category:1062 establishments in Asia]] [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Morioka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Morioka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Morioka, Iwate]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]][[co:commons:Category:Morioka-Hachimangū]][[en:Morioka Hachimangū]][[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]][[ja:盛岡八幡宮]][[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]][[co:commons:Category:Morioka-Hachimangū]][[en:Morioka Hachimangū]][[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]][[ja:盛岡八幡宮]][[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]][[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Morioka-Hachimangū]][[en:Morioka Hachimangū]][[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]][[ja:盛岡八幡宮]][[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Morioka Hachiman Shrine

[https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC| 399449}} ==External links== {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} * {{official website|http://www.morioka8man.jp/ }} {{in lang|ja}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{translated page|ja|盛岡八幡宮|version=101918390|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Morioka Hachimangū]] [[ja:盛岡八幡宮]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]] {{Commons category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} [[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]] {{Commons category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} [[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] {{wikidata link|Q11581351}} [[Category:1062 establishments in Asia]] [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Morioka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Morioka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Morioka, Iwate]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]][[co:commons:Category:Morioka-Hachimangū]][[en:Morioka Hachimangū]][[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]][[ja:盛岡八幡宮]][[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]][[co:commons:Category:Morioka-Hachimangū]][[en:Morioka Hachimangū]][[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]][[ja:盛岡八幡宮]][[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]][[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Morioka-Hachimangū]][[en:Morioka Hachimangū]][[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]][[ja:盛岡八幡宮]][[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Morioka Hachiman Shrine
盛岡八幡宮 | image = 171104 Morioka-Hachimangu Morioka Iwate pref Japan04s3.jpg | caption = Morioka Hachimangu Honden | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|39|41|43.11|N|141|09|50.62|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] | deity = [[Hachiman]] | founded_by = | established = 1062 | date_destroyed = | location = Yawata-cho 13-2, Morioka, Iwate | website = {{URL|http://www.morioka8man.jp/}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|qid=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri|1=Hachiman-zukuri}} | festival = September 15 | leadership = }} {{Nihongo|'''Morioka Hachimangū'''|盛岡八幡宮}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the city of [[:en:Morioka, Iwate|Morioka, Iwate]] in northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The shrine is noted for its [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] on the second Saturday in June, which is famous for the ''{{ill|Chagu Chagu Umakko|en|Chagu Chagu Umakko|qid=Q289513|lt=Chagu Chagu Umakko|1=Chagu Chagu Umakko}}'', a horse parade which was recognized in 1978 as an [[:en:Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Intangible Folk Cultural Property]].{{cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/312/198 |title=Database of National Cultural Properties |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=18 April 2011}} In 1996 the sound of the bells of the ''Chagu Chagu Umakko'' was selected by the {{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|en|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|qid=Q1125558|lt=Ministry of the Environment|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)}} as one of the {{ill|100 Soundscapes of Japan|en|100 Soundscapes of Japan|qid=Q1010668|lt=100 Soundscapes of Japan|1=100 Soundscapes of Japan}}.{{cite web |url=http://www.env.go.jp/air/life/oto/ |title=北海道・東北 - チャグチャグ馬コの鈴の音 |publisher={{ill|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|en|Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|qid=Q1125558|lt=Ministry of the Environment|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)}} |accessdate=18 April 2011 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120608002719/http://www.env.go.jp/air/life/oto/ |archivedate=8 June 2012 }} The shrine is also noted for its displays of ''[[yabusame]]'' horse archery during its annual festival on September 15. ==History== The Morioka Hachimangū was established in 1062 during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} when [[Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] brought a ''[[bunrei]]'' of the [[Minamoto clan]]’s tutelary shrine, the [[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] in Kyoto to pray for victory in his campaign against the [[Abe clan]] in the [[:en:Former Nine Years War|Former Nine Years War]]. It was originally called the {{nihongo|Hatomori Hachimangū|鳩森八幡宮||}}. The shrine was rebuilt in 1593 by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|en|Nanbu clan|qid=Q2975330|lt=Nanbu clan|1=Nanbu clan}} to be the protective shrine for {{ill|Morioka Castle|en|Morioka Castle|qid=Q251650|lt=Morioka Castle|1=Morioka Castle}}. Under the {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|qid=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}} system of [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|shrine Ranking]] from 1871 through 1946, the Morioka Hachimangū was officially designated as a "prefectural shrine". The Edo-period shrine structures burned down in 1884. The present main structure dates from 2006. ==Shinto belief== The shrine is dedicated to the veneration of the [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'' [[Hachiman]]. Hachiman has been recognized as an amalgamation of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Ojin|Emperor Ojin]] and his consort, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}.Ponsbonby-Fane, ''Studies,'' pp. 78, 196. ==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2362-7}}; {{OCLC|43487317}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|qid=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|194887}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|qid=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC| 399449}} ==External links== {{commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} * {{official website|http://www.morioka8man.jp/ }} {{in lang|ja}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \\ {{translated page|ja|盛岡八幡宮|version=101918390|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Morioka Hachimangū]] [[ja:盛岡八幡宮]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]] {{Commons category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} [[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] [[arz:ضريح موريوكا هاتشيمان]] {{Commons category|Morioka-Hachimangū}} [[fr:Morioka Hachiman-gū]] [[th:ศาลเจ้าโมริโอกะฮาจิมัง]] {{wikidata link|Q11581351}} [[Category:1062 establishments in Asia]] [[Category:11th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Morioka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Morioka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Morioka, Iwate]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Iwate Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
